violations.description: Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee observed handling ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Use utensils are food service gloves when handling/assembling ready to eat food items.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw ground beef stored with and above ready to eat items in kitchen refrigerator.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Particle board shelving not properly sealed in basement and BOH storage areas.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Upright freezers need to be defrosted.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Particleboard shelving units stained. Uncleanable.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. Store single service items (cups, napkins etc.) off floors in basement and BOG storage areas

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. Items stored in hand sink.--Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. Remove food items from hand sink to encourage proper hand washing.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not stored in a manner to protect against contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items. Cleaning chemical bottles stored on same table as ice scoop and ice bags in basement storage area. Cleaning chemical bottles stored on shelving unit next to and above food service items.
